records.ety: [L. diatriba a learned discussion, Gr. <?/, prop., a wearing away of time, fr. <?/ to rub away, spend time; dia` through + <?/ to rub: cf. L. terere, F. trite: cf. F. diatribe.]

defs: A prolonged or exhaustive discussion; especially, an acrimonious or invective harangue; a strain of abusive or railing language; a philippic.
quotes: The ephemeral diatribe of a faction.
